title: AP U.S. History MCQ Practice Exam 06

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that Native societies of the Pacific Northwest before sustained European contact developed complex communities around abundant coastal resources, cedar forests, plank houses, canoe travel, and ceremonial exchange. Which evidence would best support this interpretation?

choices.A: Records showing Spanish silver shipments from Mexico to Europe
choices.B: Archaeological evidence of large plank houses, carved cedar objects, salmon processing, and coastal trade goods
choices.C: English laws regulating tobacco exports from Virginia
choices.D: Maps showing cotton plantations in the lower Mississippi Valley

questions.correctAnswerText: Archaeological evidence of large plank houses, carved cedar objects, salmon processing, and coastal trade goods
questions.explanation: These forms of evidence directly support claims about Pacific Northwest environmental adaptation, resource use, and social complexity.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that Pacific Northwest Native societies built large communities around salmon, cedar, canoes, plank houses, and ceremonial exchange before sustained European contact. Which environmental factor most directly supported this pattern?

choices.A: Dry grasslands suited mainly to bison hunting
choices.B: Desert landscapes requiring irrigation agriculture
choices.C: Tropical plantation zones suited to sugar production
choices.D: Rich marine resources and forests that supported fishing, woodworking, and trade

questions.correctAnswerText: Rich marine resources and forests that supported fishing, woodworking, and trade
questions.explanation: Salmon, coastal resources, and cedar forests helped sustain large communities and specialized material culture.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that Pacific Northwest Native societies developed large plank-house communities, canoe travel, ceremonial exchange, and resource-based social hierarchies before sustained European contact. Which argument about precontact Native North America is best supported?

choices.A: Native societies adapted differently to regional environments and could develop complex social systems without European influence.
choices.B: Native peoples depended on European tools before creating permanent communities.
choices.C: Native societies across North America had identical economies and political structures.
choices.D: Coastal Native communities avoided exchange and ceremonial life before colonization.

questions.correctAnswerText: Native societies adapted differently to regional environments and could develop complex social systems without European influence.
questions.explanation: The interpretation emphasizes regional adaptation and precontact complexity independent of European colonization.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text comparison chart of plantation law and labor.
Region or colony | Labor and legal pattern
Barbados, mid-1600s | Sugar plantations; harsh slave codes; African majority population
South Carolina, late 1600s–early 1700s | Rice plantations; enslaved African labor; legal rules influenced by Caribbean models
New England, same period | Smaller enslaved population; mixed economy; port trade
Which conclusion is best supported by the chart?

choices.A: New England developed the same plantation system as Barbados.
choices.B: South Carolina avoided influence from Caribbean plantation societies.
choices.C: Caribbean plantation models helped shape slavery and law in parts of British North America.
choices.D: Slave codes weakened plantation control over enslaved labor.

questions.correctAnswerText: Caribbean plantation models helped shape slavery and law in parts of British North America.
questions.explanation: The chart connects Barbados and South Carolina through plantation labor, slave codes, and Caribbean influence.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text comparison chart of plantation law and labor. Barbados is described as having sugar plantations, harsh slave codes, and an African majority population; South Carolina as having rice plantations, enslaved African labor, and legal rules influenced by Caribbean models. Which migration pattern helped connect the two regions?

choices.A: Movement of some English planters and enslaved Africans from the Caribbean to Carolina
choices.B: Migration of Puritan families from Massachusetts to Barbados
choices.C: Forced removal of Cherokee families to Barbados
choices.D: Migration of German farmers into Spanish missions

questions.correctAnswerText: Movement of some English planters and enslaved Africans from the Caribbean to Carolina
questions.explanation: Migration from the Caribbean helped transmit plantation models and slave codes to Carolina.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text comparison chart of plantation law and labor. Barbados is linked to sugar, South Carolina to rice, and both to enslaved African labor and coercive slave law. Which claim is best supported by the chart?

choices.A: Plantation economies depended mainly on wage labor by European artisans.
choices.B: Atlantic plantation production relied on coerced African labor and legal systems designed to control that labor.
choices.C: Rice cultivation eliminated the demand for enslaved labor in South Carolina.
choices.D: British colonial law generally protected enslaved people from plantation discipline.

questions.correctAnswerText: Atlantic plantation production relied on coerced African labor and legal systems designed to control that labor.
questions.explanation: The chart links sugar, rice, enslaved African labor, and coercive legal structures.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text comparison chart of plantation law and labor. Barbados and South Carolina are linked by plantation production, enslaved African labor, and coercive slave codes, while New England is described as a smaller-slave-population, mixed-economy port region. Which broader Atlantic development does the chart illustrate?

choices.A: The end of African slavery in British colonies before 1700
choices.B: The isolation of mainland colonies from Caribbean commerce
choices.C: The decline of plantation exports in the seventeenth century
choices.D: The spread of plantation slavery through interconnected Atlantic migration, law, and trade

questions.correctAnswerText: The spread of plantation slavery through interconnected Atlantic migration, law, and trade
questions.explanation: The comparison shows plantation slavery developing through Atlantic connections among colonies and regions.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, 1787. The map shows the Northwest Territory north of the Ohio River, townships divided into rectangular grids, reserved sections for public schools, and a label noting that slavery is prohibited in the territory. Which conclusion is best supported by the map?

choices.A: The Northwest Ordinance organized western settlement while linking land policy, education, and slavery restriction.
choices.B: The federal government abandoned authority over western lands.
choices.C: The Northwest Territory was reserved exclusively for Native nations.
choices.D: Slavery was expanded by federal law into all western territories.

questions.correctAnswerText: The Northwest Ordinance organized western settlement while linking land policy, education, and slavery restriction.
questions.explanation: The map’s townships, school lands, and slavery ban all reflect key provisions of the Northwest Ordinance.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, 1787. The map shows the Northwest Territory north of the Ohio River, township grids, school land reservations, and a prohibition of slavery in the territory. Which political development is most directly reflected?

choices.A: The creation of a permanent colonial empire ruled directly by Britain
choices.B: The abolition of state governments in the early republic
choices.C: The Confederation Congress’s attempt to create an orderly process for territorial settlement and statehood
choices.D: The immediate annexation of Texas

questions.correctAnswerText: The Confederation Congress’s attempt to create an orderly process for territorial settlement and statehood
questions.explanation: The Northwest Ordinance established a process for governing western territories and admitting new states.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, 1787. The map shows the Northwest Territory divided into townships, public-school lands, and a ban on slavery north of the Ohio River. Which broader challenge facing the early republic is most directly represented?

choices.A: Deciding whether to restore monarchy
choices.B: Creating republican institutions for western expansion while balancing sectional and federal concerns
choices.C: Ending all westward migration after independence
choices.D: Building an overseas empire in the Pacific

questions.correctAnswerText: Creating republican institutions for western expansion while balancing sectional and federal concerns
questions.explanation: The early republic had to organize western land, schools, future states, and slavery policy.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, 1787. The map shows the Northwest Territory north of the Ohio River, township grids, public-school lands, and a ban on slavery. Which later conflict reflected a similar issue raised by the map?

choices.A: The debate over whether to create the Environmental Protection Agency
choices.B: The debate over whether the United States should enter World War I
choices.C: The debate over whether factory workers should receive shorter hours
choices.D: The debate over slavery’s status in new western territories

questions.correctAnswerText: The debate over slavery’s status in new western territories
questions.explanation: The Northwest Ordinance’s slavery restriction anticipated later conflicts over slavery in western territories.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated state constitutional debate excerpt, 1776–1780, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “If power returns to a single house or governor, we have changed names but not danger. A bill of rights, frequent elections, and written limits are the safeguards of liberty.” Which claim is best supported by the excerpt?

choices.A: Revolutionary state constitution writers rejected written limits on government.
choices.B: State governments after independence restored royal governors.
choices.C: Revolutionary political culture emphasized written protections against concentrated power.
choices.D: Most state constitution writers opposed elections.

questions.correctAnswerText: Revolutionary political culture emphasized written protections against concentrated power.
questions.explanation: The excerpt’s support for bills of rights, frequent elections, and written limits reflects republican fears of concentrated authority.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated state constitutional debate excerpt, 1776–1780, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The speaker calls for a bill of rights, frequent elections, and written limits to prevent concentrated power. Which historical situation best explains this concern?

choices.A: Revolutionary distrust of monarchy and arbitrary authority shaped new state governments.
choices.B: Industrial workers demanded national labor unions.
choices.C: New Deal officials expanded federal welfare programs.
choices.D: Southern states defended secession in 1861.

questions.correctAnswerText: Revolutionary distrust of monarchy and arbitrary authority shaped new state governments.
questions.explanation: Revolutionary experiences with imperial authority encouraged constitutional limits and rights protections.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated state constitutional debate excerpt, 1776–1780, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The speaker argues that “a bill of rights, frequent elections, and written limits” are safeguards of liberty. Which argument about Revolutionary political change is best supported?

choices.A: Independence produced no experiments in republican government.
choices.B: State constitution making focused only on foreign diplomacy.
choices.C: Revolutionary leaders agreed that executive power should be unlimited.
choices.D: The Revolution encouraged experiments in limiting government through written constitutions and rights protections.

questions.correctAnswerText: The Revolution encouraged experiments in limiting government through written constitutions and rights protections.
questions.explanation: State constitution making reflected republican experiments in written limits and popular accountability.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of Erie Canal effects.
Indicator | Before canal completion | After canal completion
Travel time between Buffalo and New York City | Much longer | Much shorter
Freight cost per ton | High | Lower
Western farm access to eastern markets | Limited | Expanded
Growth of canal towns | Modest | Rapid
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The Erie Canal weakened ties between western farmers and eastern markets.
choices.B: Internal improvements reduced transportation costs and helped integrate regional markets.
choices.C: Canal construction ended urban growth in New York.
choices.D: Transportation change had little effect on agricultural commerce.

questions.correctAnswerText: Internal improvements reduced transportation costs and helped integrate regional markets.
questions.explanation: The table shows falling freight costs, faster travel, wider market access, and town growth after the canal.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of Erie Canal effects. After canal completion, travel time and freight costs between Buffalo and New York City fell, western farm access to eastern markets expanded, and canal towns grew rapidly. Which geographic feature made the canal especially significant?

choices.A: It linked the Great Lakes region to the Hudson River and Atlantic trade.
choices.B: It connected California gold fields to the Pacific Ocean.
choices.C: It carried plantation cotton directly to New Orleans.
choices.D: It moved settlers across the Rocky Mountains.

questions.correctAnswerText: It linked the Great Lakes region to the Hudson River and Atlantic trade.
questions.explanation: The Erie Canal’s significance came from connecting western producers to New York and Atlantic markets.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of Erie Canal effects. Freight costs fell, western farm access to eastern markets expanded, and canal towns grew after completion of the Erie Canal. Which broader development belongs to the same context?

choices.A: Expansion of settlement and commercial farming in the Old Northwest
choices.B: Decline of all transportation investment after 1800
choices.C: Isolation of western farmers from market exchange
choices.D: Creation of overseas colonies in the Caribbean

questions.correctAnswerText: Expansion of settlement and commercial farming in the Old Northwest
questions.explanation: Cheaper transportation encouraged migration, commercial farming, and market integration in the Old Northwest.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of Erie Canal effects. The table shows lower freight costs, faster travel, expanded western farm access to eastern markets, and growth of canal towns. Which later development reflected a similar federal or state effort to reshape economic geography through transportation?

choices.A: The Alien and Sedition Acts
choices.B: The Dred Scott decision
choices.C: The National Origins Act
choices.D: Transcontinental railroad construction

questions.correctAnswerText: Transcontinental railroad construction
questions.explanation: Like canals, railroads reshaped economic geography by linking distant regions and markets.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated asylum reform petition, 1840s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “Those confined for poverty, illness, or disability should not be chained beside criminals. The state that punishes cruelty in private homes must also inspect its own institutions.” Which reform goal is most directly reflected in the petition?

choices.A: Ending all state responsibility for social welfare
choices.B: Improving prisons, asylums, and public institutions through moral reform
choices.C: Expanding plantation slavery into western territories
choices.D: Creating overseas missionary colonies

questions.correctAnswerText: Improving prisons, asylums, and public institutions through moral reform
questions.explanation: The petition calls for humane treatment and state inspection of institutions.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated asylum reform petition, 1840s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The petition argues that people confined for poverty, illness, or disability should not be chained beside criminals and that the state should inspect its own institutions. The petition’s audience was most likely which group?

choices.A: State legislators and reform-minded citizens
choices.B: British officials enforcing the Stamp Act
choices.C: Railroad executives building western lines
choices.D: NATO military planners

questions.correctAnswerText: State legislators and reform-minded citizens
questions.explanation: The petition asks the state to inspect institutions, making legislators and reform-minded publics the likely audience.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated asylum reform petition, 1840s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The petition asks the state to inspect public institutions and treat confined people humanely. Which broader antebellum development does this reflect?

choices.A: The rejection of all organized reform movements
choices.B: The decline of women’s participation in public reform
choices.C: The belief among reformers that government and moral action could improve society
choices.D: The disappearance of evangelical influence in public life

questions.correctAnswerText: The belief among reformers that government and moral action could improve society
questions.explanation: Antebellum reformers often sought to improve institutions through moral persuasion and public policy.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Free Soil campaign handbill, 1848, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “Let western lands be open to free labor, not monopolized by slave power. The settler with his own hands should not compete with a system that degrades labor and rules politics.” Which issue is most directly reflected in the handbill?

choices.A: Whether the United States should build canals
choices.B: Whether tariffs should fund federal pensions
choices.C: Whether immigrants should be excluded from cities
choices.D: Whether slavery should expand into western territories

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether slavery should expand into western territories
questions.explanation: The handbill’s “free labor” and “slave power” language centers on slavery’s expansion westward.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Free Soil campaign handbill, 1848, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The handbill contrasts “free labor” with “slave power” and argues that western settlers should not compete with slave labor. Which audience was the handbill most likely designed to persuade?

choices.A: Northern voters and western settlers concerned about labor opportunity and slavery’s expansion
choices.B: Spanish missionaries in New Mexico
choices.C: Southern secession delegates after Lincoln’s election
choices.D: Federal officials administering Japanese American incarceration

questions.correctAnswerText: Northern voters and western settlers concerned about labor opportunity and slavery’s expansion
questions.explanation: Free Soil rhetoric appealed to voters who feared slavery would threaten western free labor.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Free Soil campaign handbill, 1848, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The handbill says western lands should be open to “free labor” and not “monopolized by slave power.” Which claim is best supported?

choices.A: Free Soil supporters demanded immediate racial equality in every state.
choices.B: Free Soil politics focused only on abolishing slavery where it already existed.
choices.C: Antislavery politics could combine moral opposition to slavery with concerns about white labor and western opportunity.
choices.D: Free Soil supporters rejected all western settlement.

questions.correctAnswerText: Antislavery politics could combine moral opposition to slavery with concerns about white labor and western opportunity.
questions.explanation: The handbill frames slavery as both a moral and labor competition issue.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated Free Soil campaign handbill, 1848, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The handbill argues that western lands should be reserved for free labor rather than slave labor. Which later development most directly built on this political language?

choices.A: The creation of the Social Gospel movement
choices.B: The rise of the Republican Party on a platform opposing slavery’s expansion
choices.C: The Great Migration to northern cities
choices.D: The passage of the Immigration and Nationality Act of 1965

questions.correctAnswerText: The rise of the Republican Party on a platform opposing slavery’s expansion
questions.explanation: Free Soil ideas helped shape later Republican opposition to slavery’s expansion.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated wartime newspaper editorial, 1863, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “The rebellion threatens the nation, but a citizen arrested without trial asks whether Union can be saved by silencing the law. Emergency may be real, yet liberty must know its limits.” Which issue is most directly reflected in the editorial?

choices.A: Whether the federal government should annex Hawaii
choices.B: Whether western territories should use popular sovereignty
choices.C: Whether freedpeople should receive land redistribution
choices.D: Wartime debate over habeas corpus, civil liberties, and executive power

questions.correctAnswerText: Wartime debate over habeas corpus, civil liberties, and executive power
questions.explanation: The editorial’s concern with arrest without trial during rebellion reflects Civil War civil-liberties debates.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated wartime newspaper editorial, 1863, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The editorial says rebellion threatens the nation but warns that citizens arrested without trial raise questions about liberty. Which wartime action belongs to this context?

choices.A: Suspension of habeas corpus in some areas
choices.B: Ratification of the Nineteenth Amendment
choices.C: Creation of national parks in the West
choices.D: Passage of the National Origins Act

questions.correctAnswerText: Suspension of habeas corpus in some areas
questions.explanation: Lincoln’s administration suspended habeas corpus in some areas during the Civil War.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated wartime newspaper editorial, 1863, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The editorial debates whether the Union can be saved while limiting trial rights during rebellion. Which broader context best explains the debate?

choices.A: The decline of federal power during the Civil War
choices.B: The absence of dissent in the North
choices.C: Civil War emergency powers created tension between preserving the Union and protecting constitutional liberties.
choices.D: The Supreme Court’s decision in Brown v. Board of Education

questions.correctAnswerText: Civil War emergency powers created tension between preserving the Union and protecting constitutional liberties.
questions.explanation: The rebellion created debates about executive authority and civil liberties.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated wartime newspaper editorial, 1863, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The editorial states that emergency may be real but that “liberty must know its limits.” Which argument about the Civil War is best supported?

choices.A: The Civil War ended all disputes over civil liberties.
choices.B: The war forced Americans to debate how far government power could expand during national crisis.
choices.C: Civil liberties concerns appeared only after World War II.
choices.D: Wartime newspapers uniformly supported every federal policy.

questions.correctAnswerText: The war forced Americans to debate how far government power could expand during national crisis.
questions.explanation: The editorial shows wartime tension between national survival and individual liberties.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that late nineteenth-century segregation worked through state law, local custom, violence, and court rulings that turned public space into a system of racial hierarchy. Which evidence would best support this interpretation?

choices.A: State laws requiring separate railway cars and court decisions upholding “separate but equal”
choices.B: Federal laws guaranteeing equal access to all schools after 1877
choices.C: New Deal labor laws protecting industrial unions
choices.D: Immigration quotas favoring northern and western Europe

questions.correctAnswerText: State laws requiring separate railway cars and court decisions upholding “separate but equal”
questions.explanation: These legal measures support the interpretation that segregation was enforced through law and courts.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that late nineteenth-century segregation used law, custom, violence, and court rulings to turn public space into a system of racial hierarchy. Which Supreme Court ruling most directly belongs to this context?

choices.A: Marbury v. Madison
choices.B: Dred Scott v. Sandford
choices.C: Brown v. Board of Education
choices.D: Plessy v. Ferguson

questions.correctAnswerText: Plessy v. Ferguson
questions.explanation: Plessy v. Ferguson upheld the doctrine of “separate but equal” in public accommodations.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that late nineteenth-century segregation worked through law, custom, violence, and court rulings that turned public space into racial hierarchy. Which broader context best explains this development?

choices.A: Federal enforcement of Reconstruction protections grew stronger after 1890.
choices.B: Southern white leaders rebuilt racial control after Reconstruction while claiming local authority and social order.
choices.C: The Fourteenth Amendment immediately ended all public discrimination.
choices.D: Segregation was limited to northern industrial cities.

questions.correctAnswerText: Southern white leaders rebuilt racial control after Reconstruction while claiming local authority and social order.
questions.explanation: After Reconstruction, segregation and disfranchisement helped restore white supremacy in the South.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that late nineteenth-century segregation used law, custom, violence, and court rulings to create racial hierarchy in public space. Which argument is best supported?

choices.A: Segregation emerged only from private prejudice and had no legal basis.
choices.B: Segregation expanded Black political power in the South.
choices.C: Law and everyday custom worked together to define unequal citizenship after Reconstruction.
choices.D: Court rulings consistently protected integrated public accommodations.

questions.correctAnswerText: Law and everyday custom worked together to define unequal citizenship after Reconstruction.
questions.explanation: The interpretation emphasizes both legal structures and social practices in maintaining racial hierarchy.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map and strike notice, 1894. The map shows rail lines radiating from Chicago across the Midwest and West. A notice from railway workers asks members not to handle Pullman cars until wages and company-town rents are addressed. Which conflict is most directly reflected?

choices.A: The Pullman Strike and the national importance of railroad labor
choices.B: The Boston Tea Party and resistance to imperial taxation
choices.C: The Montgomery Bus Boycott and public transportation segregation
choices.D: The debate over the Louisiana Purchase

questions.correctAnswerText: The Pullman Strike and the national importance of railroad labor
questions.explanation: The map, Chicago rail lines, Pullman cars, wages, and company-town rents all point to the Pullman Strike.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map and strike notice, 1894. Rail lines radiate from Chicago across the Midwest and West, and a worker notice urges members not to handle Pullman cars until wages and company-town rents are addressed. Which feature of the map helps explain why the strike became a national issue?

choices.A: Rail lines were restricted to one town in Illinois.
choices.B: Chicago had no role in national transportation.
choices.C: Interconnected rail networks linked local labor conflict to national commerce.
choices.D: Pullman workers controlled all federal courts.

questions.correctAnswerText: Interconnected rail networks linked local labor conflict to national commerce.
questions.explanation: Rail connections made a local strike disruptive to interstate commerce and national transportation.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map and strike notice, 1894. The map shows rail lines radiating from Chicago, while a worker notice calls for action against Pullman cars over wages and company-town rents. Which government response belongs to this context?

choices.A: Federal protection of all workers’ demands before negotiations
choices.B: Immediate nationalization of the railroad industry
choices.C: Federal refusal to intervene in any railroad dispute
choices.D: Use of federal injunctions and troops to restore rail traffic

questions.correctAnswerText: Use of federal injunctions and troops to restore rail traffic
questions.explanation: The federal government used injunctions and troops during the Pullman Strike to restore rail service.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated factory inspection report, 1911, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “The exits open inward, the aisles are crowded with cloth, and many girls do not know whether a fire drill exists. The law may be printed, but without inspection it is only decoration.” Which reform concern is most directly reflected in the report?

choices.A: Western land conservation and national parks
choices.B: Workplace safety and Progressive regulation of industrial conditions
choices.C: The expansion of plantation slavery
choices.D: The creation of national-origins immigration quotas

questions.correctAnswerText: Workplace safety and Progressive regulation of industrial conditions
questions.explanation: The report’s unsafe exits, crowded aisles, and lack of inspections reflect Progressive labor-safety concerns.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated factory inspection report, 1911, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The inspector warns that exits open inward, aisles are crowded, young women workers lack fire-drill knowledge, and laws mean little without inspection. The likely purpose of the report was to persuade which audience?

choices.A: Legislators and the public to support stronger workplace regulation
choices.B: British officials to repeal the Stamp Act
choices.C: Plantation owners to expand cotton production
choices.D: NATO leaders to increase defense spending

questions.correctAnswerText: Legislators and the public to support stronger workplace regulation
questions.explanation: The report’s emphasis on unenforced laws and unsafe conditions was designed to build support for regulation.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated factory inspection report, 1911, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The report describes crowded industrial conditions, young women workers, inadequate fire safety, and weak enforcement. Which broader context best explains the report?

choices.A: The disappearance of factory labor after 1900
choices.B: The federal government’s refusal to regulate any workplace after 1910
choices.C: The decline of women’s wage work in cities
choices.D: Industrial disasters and urban reform campaigns encouraged new labor and safety laws.

questions.correctAnswerText: Industrial disasters and urban reform campaigns encouraged new labor and safety laws.
questions.explanation: Progressive reformers responded to industrial dangers with inspection and workplace safety campaigns.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated factory inspection report, 1911, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The report describes unsafe conditions affecting many young women workers and calls for meaningful inspection. Which broader Progressive Era development is most closely related?

choices.A: The decline of middle-class women’s reform activism
choices.B: The elimination of all industrial accidents before regulation
choices.C: Women reformers’ participation in campaigns for labor protection and public welfare
choices.D: The end of immigrant labor in urban factories

questions.correctAnswerText: Women reformers’ participation in campaigns for labor protection and public welfare
questions.explanation: Women reformers often played major roles in factory inspection, labor protection, and public welfare campaigns.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of wartime migration and labor, 1910–1920.
Pattern | Change
African American population in selected northern cities | Increased sharply
Industrial labor demand | Increased during World War I
Southern racial violence and disfranchisement | Continued
Black newspapers | Encouraged migration and reported job opportunities
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: World War I labor demand and southern racial conditions contributed to the Great Migration.
choices.B: African American migration to northern cities ended before World War I.
choices.C: Black newspapers discouraged all movement out of the South.
choices.D: Northern industrial labor demand declined during World War I.

questions.correctAnswerText: World War I labor demand and southern racial conditions contributed to the Great Migration.
questions.explanation: The table links wartime jobs, southern racial violence and disfranchisement, newspapers, and migration.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of wartime migration and labor, 1910–1920. African American populations in selected northern cities increased sharply, industrial labor demand rose during World War I, southern racial violence and disfranchisement continued, and Black newspapers encouraged migration. Which economic factor helped create the migration pattern shown?

choices.A: The collapse of northern industry before 1914
choices.B: Wartime factory labor shortages in northern cities
choices.C: The elimination of wage labor in the Midwest
choices.D: Federal land redistribution to Black farmers in the South

questions.correctAnswerText: Wartime factory labor shortages in northern cities
questions.explanation: World War I reduced some labor supplies and increased industrial demand, encouraging Black migration north.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of wartime migration and labor, 1910–1920. African American populations in northern cities increased, wartime labor demand rose, southern racial violence and disfranchisement continued, and Black newspapers encouraged migration. Which social consequence followed from this migration?

choices.A: Immediate racial equality in all northern housing and workplaces
choices.B: The end of Black political activism in northern cities
choices.C: Growth of Black urban communities and new racial tensions in northern cities
choices.D: Elimination of all segregation in the South

questions.correctAnswerText: Growth of Black urban communities and new racial tensions in northern cities
questions.explanation: The Great Migration expanded Black urban communities and also contributed to racial tensions over jobs and housing.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of wartime migration and labor, 1910–1920. African American populations in northern cities increased, wartime labor demand rose, southern racial violence and disfranchisement continued, and Black newspapers encouraged migration. Which later development was shaped by the communities formed through this migration?

choices.A: The creation of Spanish missions in New Mexico
choices.B: The Missouri Compromise line
choices.C: The annexation of the Philippines
choices.D: The growth of Black political, cultural, and civil rights organizing in northern cities

questions.correctAnswerText: The growth of Black political, cultural, and civil rights organizing in northern cities
questions.explanation: Black urban communities shaped later political, cultural, and civil rights activism.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1970s. The photograph shows school buses outside a city school while parents hold signs with opposing messages: some demand “equal schools now,” while others protest “forced busing.” Police officers stand between groups. Which issue is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Conflicts over court-ordered school desegregation and busing
choices.B: The creation of the Freedmen’s Bureau
choices.C: The First Great Awakening
choices.D: The Dawes Act

questions.correctAnswerText: Conflicts over court-ordered school desegregation and busing
questions.explanation: The photograph’s school buses, opposing parent signs, and police presence reflect busing disputes.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1970s. School buses are parked outside a city school while parents hold signs for and against busing, and police officers stand between groups. The photograph’s historical situation is best explained by which development?

choices.A: The end of all legal segregation immediately after World War II
choices.B: Federal refusal to enforce any civil rights laws
choices.C: Court efforts to address de facto and de jure school segregation after Brown v. Board of Education
choices.D: The expansion of slavery into western territories

questions.correctAnswerText: Court efforts to address de facto and de jure school segregation after Brown v. Board of Education
questions.explanation: Busing was used in some cities as a remedy for persistent school segregation after Brown.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1970s. The image shows school buses, parents with opposing signs about busing, and police officers between groups outside a city school. Which broader development is reflected by the conflict?

choices.A: The decline of public education after the American Revolution
choices.B: Civil rights victories produced new conflicts over implementation, local control, and racial equality.
choices.C: The disappearance of racial politics in northern cities
choices.D: The end of federal courts’ role in civil rights issues

questions.correctAnswerText: Civil rights victories produced new conflicts over implementation, local control, and racial equality.
questions.explanation: The photograph shows that legal victories did not end disputes over how integration would be implemented.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1970s. Parents hold opposing signs about school busing while police separate groups outside a city school. Which argument about the civil rights era is best supported?

choices.A: Civil rights issues disappeared once national legislation passed in the 1960s.
choices.B: School integration debates were limited to the rural South.
choices.C: Federal courts avoided involvement in education after 1954.
choices.D: The struggle over racial equality continued through disputes over policy implementation and community resistance.

questions.correctAnswerText: The struggle over racial equality continued through disputes over policy implementation and community resistance.
questions.explanation: Busing controversies show continuing conflict over civil rights after major legislation and court rulings.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of U.S. economic conditions, 1970s.
Indicator | Pattern
Inflation | High
Unemployment | High
Energy prices | Rose sharply after oil shocks
Manufacturing employment | Pressured by competition and automation
Public confidence in economic management | Declined
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The 1970s economy combined inflation, unemployment, energy shocks, and industrial pressure.
choices.B: The 1970s were marked by low inflation and full employment.
choices.C: Energy prices had no effect on economic politics.
choices.D: Manufacturing employment expanded evenly in all regions.

questions.correctAnswerText: The 1970s economy combined inflation, unemployment, energy shocks, and industrial pressure.
questions.explanation: The table identifies high inflation, high unemployment, oil shocks, industrial pressure, and declining confidence.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of U.S. economic conditions, 1970s. Inflation and unemployment were high, energy prices rose after oil shocks, manufacturing faced competition and automation, and public confidence in economic management declined. Which term is most closely associated with these conditions?

choices.A: Stagflation
choices.B: Mercantilism
choices.C: Salutary neglect
choices.D: Manifest Destiny

questions.correctAnswerText: Stagflation
questions.explanation: Stagflation refers to the unusual combination of high inflation and high unemployment.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of U.S. economic conditions, 1970s. Inflation and unemployment were high, energy prices rose after oil shocks, manufacturing employment was pressured, and confidence in economic management declined. Which later political development was influenced by these conditions?

choices.A: The decline of all conservative politics after 1980
choices.B: The collapse of debates over federal economic policy
choices.C: Growing criticism of liberal economic management and support for tax cuts, deregulation, and monetary restraint
choices.D: Universal support for expanding Great Society programs without criticism

questions.correctAnswerText: Growing criticism of liberal economic management and support for tax cuts, deregulation, and monetary restraint
questions.explanation: Economic problems in the 1970s helped strengthen later critiques of liberal economic policy.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of digital commerce and work.
Pattern | Late 20th to early 21st century development
Online retail | Expanded rapidly
App-based service work | Grew in transportation, delivery, and short-term tasks
Traditional retail stores | Faced competition from online platforms
Worker classification debates | Increased over independent contractors and benefits
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Digital platforms had no effect on work or commerce.
choices.B: Online retail eliminated all service work.
choices.C: Traditional retail expanded without competition from digital platforms.
choices.D: Digital commerce reshaped consumer habits and labor debates.

questions.correctAnswerText: Digital commerce reshaped consumer habits and labor debates.
questions.explanation: The table shows online retail, app-based service work, competition with traditional stores, and contractor debates.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of digital commerce and work. Online retail expanded rapidly, app-based service work grew, traditional retail faced competition, and debates increased over independent contractors and benefits. Which group was most directly affected by the worker-classification debate shown?

choices.A: Colonial artisans protesting British taxation
choices.B: Gig workers and platform-based contractors seeking benefits or labor protections
choices.C: Enslaved laborers on sugar plantations
choices.D: Federal officials enforcing the Proclamation of 1763

questions.correctAnswerText: Gig workers and platform-based contractors seeking benefits or labor protections
questions.explanation: Worker-classification debates directly affect platform workers treated as independent contractors.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of digital commerce and work. Online retail expanded, app-based service work grew, traditional retail stores faced competition, and debates increased over contractors and benefits. Which broader economic context best explains these changes?

choices.A: The end of global supply chains after 1980
choices.B: The growth of internet technology, logistics networks, and flexible service-sector employment
choices.C: The disappearance of consumer demand for home delivery
choices.D: The return of the plantation economy

questions.correctAnswerText: The growth of internet technology, logistics networks, and flexible service-sector employment
questions.explanation: These developments made online retail and app-based work possible.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of digital commerce and work. Online retail expanded rapidly, app-based service work grew, traditional retail faced competition, and debates increased over independent contractors and benefits. Which argument about recent U.S. economic change is best supported?

choices.A: Technological change ended conflict between workers and employers.
choices.B: Digital platforms strengthened only manufacturing unions.
choices.C: New technologies created economic convenience while raising new questions about regulation, benefits, and worker rights.
choices.D: Consumer habits remained unchanged by online commerce.

questions.correctAnswerText: New technologies created economic convenience while raising new questions about regulation, benefits, and worker rights.
questions.explanation: The table shows both convenience and new labor-policy disputes.
